findings.claim: Plan's only mitigation for self-identified highest risk is 'a strict, prescriptive system-prompt rule' — zero falsifiable evidence that BYOK models will reliably honor a 120-token instruction to call set_todos before first tool call.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan says 'iterate the system prompt copy until the agent reliably calls set_todos upfront' — the success criterion is 'we tweaked it until it worked once on Henry's BYOK model.' Aegis runs on BYOK (varying capability), not a tuned model. Cursor's todo_write works because they control model+harness end-to-end. Memory note shows agent ALREADY ignores existing prompt rules (chatty self-narration).
findings.suggested_patch: Add Task 8.5: define measurable discipline metric (e.g. ≥8/10 across {OpenAI, Anthropic, Google}). Server-side fallback: detect ≥2 consecutive content-mutating tool calls without preceding set_todos and inject reminder.

findings.plan_section: Data Model > Derivation rule + Visibility rule
findings.claim: Scoping todos to 'latest assistant message only' is asserted as a feature but is a footgun: any normal multi-turn fix flow loses the in-progress strip the moment the user sends any reply.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan: 'each new user message implicitly retires the old list' and 'derive from messages[messages.length - 1]'. Aegis flows include user mid-stream interjections. If user types 'wait, also do X', the strip vanishes until next assistant turn.
findings.suggested_patch: Either (a) walk backward through assistant messages to find most recent set_todos and merge subsequent update_todo across messages until new set_todos, OR (b) explicit rule 'on any user reply, re-emit set_todos as first action.'

findings.claim: Derivation walks parts looking for type === 'tool-call' and reads 'args' — but Vercel AI SDK v5 persisted UI message parts use {type: 'tool-<toolName>', state, input, output}, not {type: 'tool-call', toolName, args}.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble already walks parts. Per AI SDK v5 / useChat UI message format docs, parts arrive as 'tool-<name>' with input/output. Plan's tests will pass against fake data shaped per the plan but the wired component will silently render nothing.
findings.suggested_patch: Before writing derivation, grep MessageBubble.tsx for how it identifies tool calls. Mirror exact pattern. Update Data Model example shapes to match SDK reality. Verification step: load one real assistant message with tool call from DB and assert deriveTodos handles its actual shape.

findings.claim: Plan assumes update_todo's args drive UI directly during streaming, but every update_todo call costs a full LLM round-trip. 5-item plan = 11 tool calls = ~10× round-trips of letting model do work + emit summary.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: BYOK at typical Aegis context (200 msg/day, 5 concurrent, monthly cost cap) means UI-animation chrome consumes paid tokens.
findings.suggested_patch: Add 'Cost & latency' bullet to Risks. Consider a single set_todos_with_progress carrying full updated list, OR batch updates (update_todos plural).

findings.claim: Success Criteria are entirely manual; no automated regression detection when system prompt drifts, BYOK model changes, or AI SDK part shape bumps — silent breakage at exactly the wrong moment.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Test plan only deriveTodos unit tests + manual dogfood. No e2e, no recorded-trace replay, no model-behavior regression.
findings.suggested_patch: Integration test recording fixture stream from real model run (or hand-crafted realistic SSE event sequence) asserting strip's render output. At minimum, Vitest test seeding messages with realistic parts array including mixed set_todos + update_todo + other tool calls + text deltas.

findings.plan_section: Risks > Conflict with existing FixPanel side panel
findings.claim: Hand-waving 'this is a copy/onboarding clarification' ignores that for the canonical use case ('revise both plans'), FixPanel is literally also showing the same plans with their own to-dos/verification rows. Users will see two near-identical lists.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan reuses SAME visual recipe ('Visual recipe matches FixListBody's To-dos / Verification cards'). Chat strip ['revise-plan-a', 'revise-plan-b'] vs side panel actual checklist for plan open. Duplicated chrome at best, contradictory at worst.
findings.suggested_patch: Define hard rule for chat strip vs side panel with worked example. Either (a) strip shows ONLY meta-orchestration, never per-plan substeps, or (b) hide side panel's to-dos when chat strip active. Add 'Surface boundaries' subsection.

findings.claim: Suppressing set_todos/update_todo from MessageBubble's dispatch covers rendering, but does not suppress the parts from feeding into ToolCallGroup count badges, message-export, search, OR the prompt history replayed back to the model on subsequent turns.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Model will see ~12 tool-call parts per multi-step turn for pure UI bookkeeping. Combined with f4, balloons context windows.
findings.suggested_patch: Backend rule: when reconstructing prior-turn context for LLM, filter out set_todos/update_todo tool-call parts (or compress to system note 'previous turn declared and completed N todos').

findings.claim: Plan ships uncertainty about its own dependency: 'Use framer-motion (already a dependency? grep first; if not, plain CSS).' 30-second grep deferred to /implement.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Resolving now changes the visual spec.
findings.suggested_patch: Grep package.json now and lock the answer in the plan.

findings.plan_section: Open questions > Reuse Aegis tasks schema
findings.claim: Open question 'Confirm with Henry' is not a resolved design — getting it wrong creates worst kind of duplication (two parallel todo systems).
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Success Criteria #1 'revise both plans' maps cleanly to Aegis's existing fix-worker plan-then-execute system per CLAUDE.md.
findings.suggested_patch: Resolve before /implement. If 'no reuse,' add: 'Chat todos do NOT reference fix-worker task ids.' If 'partial,' design the linkage.

findings.claim: 'Audit: ~120 tokens added; negligible' is asserted not measured, and sample copy in same section is materially longer than 120 tokens.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Sample copy alone reads ~140-180 tokens before tool-list line, per-tool input schema descriptions, and dogfood iteration.
findings.suggested_patch: Replace 'negligible' with measured count after writing final prompt copy. Add token cap (≤200 tokens).

findings.claim: Two tools (set_todos + update_todo) is over-engineered — one tool with full-list-replace semantics captures 100% of the value with half the schema, half the prompt budget, and removes id-validation problem entirely.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan itself notes cross-call id validation is hard and is being skipped; frontend 'ignores update_todo whose id isn't in current set_todos.' That's a tell. Cursor's todo_write is single tool taking the whole list every call. Claude Code's TodoWrite is also single-tool.
findings.suggested_patch: Collapse to one tool set_todos({ todos: [{ id?, title, status }] }) where status defaults to pending. Each call replaces active list. Derivation becomes: 'find last set_todos in latest assistant message; render its todos array.'

findings.claim: Dedicated frontend/src/lib/aegis-todos.ts helper file plus Jest test is gold-plating for ~25 lines of pure array-walking with one caller.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Helper has exactly one consumer, no shared state, no framework boundary. Inline derivation inside ChatTodos.tsx is cheaper.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop Task 4 as separate file. Inline deriveTodos as non-exported function inside ChatTodos.tsx.

findings.claim: framer-motion is overkill for opacity+8px translate fade. Plain CSS / Tailwind transitions cover this, plan is uncertain whether framer-motion is even already a dependency.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan hedges 'verify it's a dependency; fall back to plain CSS'. Just commit to plain CSS up front; smaller, no SSR/hydration quirks, matches SendQueuePanel.
findings.suggested_patch: Remove framer-motion. Use Tailwind transition classes; CSS for exit-animation.

findings.claim: reason field on set_todos has no defined consumer ('tooltip / debug') — adds prompt-budget cost on every multi-step turn for zero v1 UX.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan §Polish lists 'Tooltip-on-hover showing reason if present' with no committed UI. Model spends tokens producing it on every call.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop reason from v1 schema.

findings.claim: Requiring model to invent stable kebab-case ids per item is more cognitive load than v1 needs; index-based references would work.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: If single-tool full-replace adopted, ids unnecessary. Plan cites real risk of model fabricating ids.
findings.suggested_patch: If two tools survive, update_todo({index, status}). set_todos id becomes optional/synthetic.

findings.claim: Task 9's three polish items + open question on terminal-state hold are speculative tuning before any user has seen the strip.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Pre-launch solo-user, right move is to ship simplest behavior and tune from real dogfood.
findings.suggested_patch: Cut Task 9. Default: strip vanishes when all terminal; titles use truncate; no tooltip.

findings.claim: Modeling four states (pending / in_progress / done / cancelled) is one more state than v1 needs.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: No described user gesture or agent flow produces cancelled in v1. Three states cover every described scenario.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop cancelled from v1 enum, row table, icon mapping, prompt.

findings.claim: Six derivation cases is too many for a 5-line function and is symptomatic of the f1/f2 over-extraction.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Two tests cover it: 'no set_todos returns empty', 'last set_todos wins'. Other four only exist because of two-tool merge logic.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop derivation test count to 2 after collapsing tools. If two tools kept, cap at 4.

findings.claim: Task 9's three sub-items (1.5s 'Done.' hold, long-title truncation, hover tooltip) are post-ship polish that should be excluded from v1.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan section 'Visibility rule' already says 'TBD in design pass'. Truncation already covered by truncate min-w-0 flex-1. None needed to validate agent declares and updates a plan.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop Task 9 entirely. Render null when all todos terminal. Defer tooltip until users ask.

findings.claim: reason field has no consumer in v1 — only Task 9's deferred tooltip references it.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Derivation algorithm and ChatTodos spec never read it. Burns prompt tokens for the model to read schema description, invites model to spend output tokens generating rationales nobody sees.
findings.suggested_patch: Remove reason from set_todos jsonSchema for v1.

findings.claim: cancelled status is in enum but no flow drives the model to emit it; effectively unreachable in v1.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: System-prompt sample only describes in_progress → done transitions. Visibility rule treats cancelled same as done for hide-strip.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop cancelled from enum and TodoRow style table for v1.

findings.claim: Framer Motion initial/animate/exit on the strip is animation polish not justified for a sticky strip whose presence is already obvious.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan flags 'verify it's a dependency'. SendQueuePanel — explicitly named template — uses no motion.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop motion.div/AnimatePresence. Plain conditional + transition-opacity.

findings.claim: Six Jest cases for ~20-line pure function is more coverage than the helper's complexity earns.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Realistic failure modes are (a) no set_todos returns [] and (b) updates apply by id. Other cases are degenerate paths the user-visible flow exposes immediately.
findings.suggested_patch: Cut to 2 tests.

findings.claim: Splitting ChatTodos into its own file plus separate aegis-todos.ts helper is more files than feature warrants.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Component is ~25 lines driven by pure helper used in exactly one place.
findings.suggested_patch: Either keep helper file (testable) and inline ChatTodos JSX into ChatPane, OR keep component file and inline helper inside it.

findings.claim: 'Mid-flight redirect' and 'stop button' test prompt discipline rather than the feature itself, flagged as stretch in plan.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan annotates scenario 3 as '(Stretch)'. Scenario 5 covers existing functionality.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop scenarios 3 and 5 from v1 acceptance bar.

findings.claim: Plan correctly cuts server-side cross-call id check; flag this as right call but make sure 'soft check' line doesn't sneak back.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan still describes the heavier path before cutting it.
findings.suggested_patch: Replace two paragraphs with single line: 'execute returns {ok:true}; persistence automatic via AI SDK; frontend ignores update_todo with unknown id.'

findings.axis: shape mismatch — runtime vs persisted vs derivation
findings.claim: deriveTodos as written never matches any real part. AI SDK runtime emits type: 'tool-{name}' with input/output/state; persisted parts are converted by ChatPane.buildInitialMessages into type: 'dynamic-tool' with input/output. NEITHER shape has type: 'tool-call' in UIMessage messages array, NEITHER carries args/result fields.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble.tsx:34-47 inspects part.type === 'dynamic-tool' || part.type.startsWith('tool-') and reads part.output (not result), with toolName derived from part.type.replace('tool-',''). ChatPane.tsx:88-129 buildInitialMessages converts persisted tool-call MessagePart entries into UIMessage parts of type: 'dynamic-tool' with input/output. Rebuilt UIMessage never has type: 'tool-call' or fields named args/result.
findings.suggested_patch: Rewrite deriveTodos to mirror MessageBubble's part adapter. Pull helper logic into shared aegis-parts.ts so both files stay in lockstep.

findings.claim: Plan says framer-motion is 'most likely a dependency'. It is NOT. Frontend has zero framer-motion imports anywhere and no framer-motion entry in package.json.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Verified frontend/package.json has only tailwindcss-animate. Existing chat ambient animations use raw CSS in style props (ChatPane.tsx:603-605 'animation: aegis-thinking 1.6s ease-in-out infinite').
findings.suggested_patch: Drop framer-motion. Specify transition-opacity duration-200 plus tailwindcss-animate. Saves 60kb dependency.

findings.plan_section: Data Model / 'tool results are minimal {ok:true}; the args carry the state'
findings.claim: During streaming SDK does NOT include input on tool-call part until input streaming completes. On rehydration, ChatPane only emits UIMessage part when there is a paired tool-result.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: ChatPane.tsx:99-117: only tool-result parts produce dynamic-tool UI part; bare tool-call parts skipped. backend/src/lib/aegis-v3/parts.ts:48 if (resultByCallId.has(call.toolCallId)). Mid-stream/aborted set_todos calls won't survive refresh.
findings.suggested_patch: Either (a) walk both tool-call and tool-result parts during rehydration, or (b) explicitly accept rehydration only shows todos after corresponding {ok:true} result is persisted.

findings.plan_section: Codebase Analysis — 'Tool list line in prompt — line 43'
findings.claim: Plan says 'line 43 lists tools per category'. Actual line 43 is the Fix bullet. Folding todos into Fix category would mislead model into treating set_todos as fix-only tool.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: system-prompt.ts:35-43 lists Teams/Projects/People/Roles/Security/Issues/Intelligence/Policy/Fix; rule 9 is fix-specific.
findings.suggested_patch: Add new bullet (Plan / Orchestration) and create numbered rule (10) explicitly NOT scoped to fixes.

findings.axis: scope drift in rule for which message owns todos
findings.claim: Plan says 'derive todos from latest assistant message's parts' but latest message after a finished turn is often a user message. Using messages[messages.length - 1] returns [] on every user follow-up; strip flickers out at every send.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan implementation sketch contradicts visibility rule.
findings.suggested_patch: Walk backwards from messages[length-1], pick most recent role==='assistant' message. Define turn boundary rule in spec.

findings.claim: Plan says 'early-return on toolName === set_todos|update_todo'. MessageBubble forEach-iterates parts and pushes; there is no per-message early-return.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble.tsx:99 parts.forEach((part, i) => {...}); push to toolBuffer.
findings.suggested_patch: Specify literally: inside isToolPart branch, after computing toolName, if (toolName === 'set_todos' || toolName === 'update_todo') return; (forEach return skips iteration). Add unit test fixture.

findings.plan_section: API Design — server-side execute cross-call id validation
findings.claim: Plan suggests cross-call id validation 'if AI SDK exposes prior parts'. AegisToolContext is {orgId, userId, threadId, operatingMode, supabase} — no parts history.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: tool-types.ts:15-21 AegisToolContext shape; no toolset entry receives stream/turn history.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop 'optionally validate' fork. Plan v1 = pure pass-through {ok:true}.

findings.plan_section: Codebase Analysis — FixPanel visual recipe citation
findings.claim: Plan cites specific FixPanel.tsx line range as visual template; should be grep-verified before /implement per Henry's standing rule.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan line 243; not verified. Henry historically flagged unverified line citations as P0 (Phase 6.5).
findings.suggested_patch: Read FixPanel.tsx:372-432 and confirm in plan or correct range.

findings.claim: Proposed deriveTodos and unit tests written against parts[i].type === 'tool-call' but actual SDK part shape is type === 'tool-<name>' (or dynamic-tool) — tests pass against wrong fixture shape while runtime never matches.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble.tsx:34-47 isToolPart matches dynamic-tool or type.startsWith('tool-'); toolNameFor strips tool- prefix. Nowhere is type === 'tool-call' valid.
findings.suggested_patch: Fix algorithm spec to mirror MessageBubble's isToolPart + toolNameFor. Add contract test loading fixture captured from actual stream/save round-trip.

findings.claim: Plan reads args.todos but Vercel AI SDK persists tool-call inputs under 'input' (and 'output' for results). No test verifies actual on-disk JSON shape of saved tool-call part.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble.tsx:107 uses part.output (not result) — consistent with AI SDK v5 part shape {type: 'tool-<name>', input, output, state}.
findings.suggested_patch: Add backend round-trip test in aegis-v3-stream.test.ts: simulate stream emitting set_todos tool call and assert row saved to aegis_chat_messages.metadata.parts contains entry with exact key frontend will read.

findings.claim: Plan hedges 'existing prompt-snapshot tests (if any) get updated' and falls back to manual streaming inspection. Prompt-assertion tests DO exist.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: backend/src/__tests__/aegis-v3-helpers.test.ts:131 'embeds the org name and the never-invent-IDs guardrail'.
findings.suggested_patch: Add explicit task: extend buildAegisSystemPrompt test with new it('includes the multi-step todos rule') asserting prompt contains set_todos and update_todo and the rule-governing phrase.

findings.claim: Plan asserts execute is pass-through returning {ok:true} and never mandates input-validation test, despite non-trivial schema constraints.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Existing aegis-v3-tools.test.ts:100 has pattern 'no read-only tool advertises a UUID-formatted arg' — schema-level assertions are convention.
findings.suggested_patch: Add to aegis-v3-tools.test.ts: schema rejects empty array, >12 items, missing fields, additional props. Status enum validated.

findings.claim: Plan describes ChatTodos as 'optional smoke render' even though frontend has working Vitest setup with 13 component tests. 'Optional smoke' is below existing project bar.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: frontend/src/__tests__/ has 13 test files including incident-response-ui.test.tsx, security-slas-ui.test.tsx.
findings.suggested_patch: Promote component test from 'optional' to 'required'. Add aegis-chat-todos.test.tsx with 8 cases covering icon mapping, header counter, visibility rule.

findings.claim: Task 7 modifies MessageBubble to early-return; plan says 'verify request_fix/revise_fix/approve_fix flows still render correctly' but lists no automated regression test.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble.tsx:84-145 has 4 distinct rendering branches plus flushTools buffer; no MessageBubble.test.tsx exists.
findings.suggested_patch: Add frontend/src/__tests__/message-bubble.test.tsx with cases per existing branch.

findings.claim: Headline outcome 'page refresh rehydrates strip' is verify-only (manual scenario 4). Every future change to message persistence silently risks regressing.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Backend stream test asserts persisted parts shape — precedent exists, not used.
findings.suggested_patch: Add integration test seeding aegis_chat_messages row, loading through frontend code path, running deriveTodos, asserting Todo[].

findings.axis: dogfood scenarios insufficient for declared highest risk
findings.claim: Plan flags model discipline as highest risk; 5 dogfood scenarios all positive paths. None tests model invents id, calls set_todos twice, calls update_todo without set_todos, borderline cases.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan dogfood scenarios at lines 329-334 cover single-step (negative), multi-revise, mid-flight redirect (stretch), refresh, stop button — single-pass.
findings.suggested_patch: Replace 5-scenario list with structured matrix: [single, 2-action, 3+ action, ambiguous] x [happy, mid-redirect, error, user stops]. Run each cell ≥3x for determinism.

findings.claim: Plan documents 'frontend ignores update_todo with unknown id' as v1 simplification; silent-skip path is exactly where highest-risk failure ends up — and it's untested.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan line 191-194; line 288 lists unknown-id case but only as derivation pure-function unit, no telemetry.
findings.suggested_patch: Either (a) extend test to assert console.warn fires on unknown id, or (b) ship dev-only debug overlay listing 'orphan update_todo calls'.

findings.claim: Task 2 acceptance phrased as if test existence is uncertain; it DOES exist. When tools register, that exact assertion needs updating.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: aegis-v3-tools.test.ts:59-100 has explicit name-list assertions of registry.
findings.suggested_patch: Change Task 2 acceptance to: extend registry name-list assertion to include set_todos and update_todo, asserting both safe/no-permission.

findings.claim: Plan claims '<1ms for typical (≤8 todos, ≤30 parts)' — no test.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan lines 263-265 and 337.
findings.suggested_patch: Drop the '<1ms' claim or add micro-benchmark in aegis-todos.test.ts asserting <50ms for 50-part array x 1000.

findings.claim: Plan calls model discipline highest risk but proposes only manual dogfood judgment to measure it. Without numeric telemetry, the iterate-the-prompt loop has no signal.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Cheap to add structured log line per turn. Fields: thread_id, message_id, set_todos_count, update_todo_count, terminal_count, abandoned_count, expected_multistep heuristic.
findings.suggested_patch: In chat.ts execute() emit console.info(JSON.stringify({event:'aegis_todo', tool, threadId, messageId, count})). Grep one week of logs to compute discipline rate before iterating prompt copy.

findings.claim: Each todo row currently renders title text only. Making row click-jump to finding/plan/project surface is 5-line addition turning strip from progress chrome into navigation aid.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Schema supports it: extend set_todos input item with optional link?: { kind: 'finding'|'fix'|'plan'|'project', handle: string }. Cursor v1.5 added the same affordance.
findings.suggested_patch: Add link?: { kind, handle } (optional) to set_todos jsonSchema. Render TodoRow as button calling existing nav handler.

findings.claim: Plan carves out FixPanel but Fix Agent already maintains its own todo list. Surfacing 'View in FixPanel →' on a todo whose link.kind === 'fix' is cheapest bridge.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Without this, dogfooders see two parallel progress lists.
findings.suggested_patch: When link.kind === 'fix', render tiny ArrowUpRight icon at row's right edge that calls FixPanel-open handler.

findings.plan_section: Overview / why not the heavier task path
findings.claim: Fix-worker streams progress via QStash; if it emitted update_todo to the chat thread when long-running fix associated with chat turn, user sees fix progress live in same strip.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Fix-worker already writes back to aegis_chat_messages. Defer for v1.1 but call out so v1 schema doesn't preclude it.
findings.suggested_patch: Add note: 'Schema is intentionally agent-agnostic — fix-worker can emit update_todo parts in future iteration without schema change.'

findings.plan_section: Open questions > Reuse Aegis tasks schema
findings.claim: Cheapest hedge: log model_id per (orgId) so we can answer 'do todos appear more often with Sonnet vs Gemini?' in a month.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: One extra field on log line from f1.
findings.suggested_patch: In Task 8, include model_id and provider in structured log on every set_todos call.

findings.claim: Plan limits todos to multi-step fix/revise. Same primitive generalizes — calling that out in prompt rule (one extra example) costs ~10 tokens.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Without this, dogfood sample-size for measuring discipline is small.
findings.suggested_patch: Add one more example in sample copy: ('triage these 5 findings', 'list and review every project status', 'audit org admin roles').

findings.claim: On mobile / small viewports, 12-item strip doesn't collapse.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: schema explicitly allows 12 items; on viewports <640px the strip eats screen.
findings.suggested_patch: Add Layout note: 'On viewports <640px collapse to single-row summary that expands on tap.'

findings.claim: One-line tweak ('walk back through last N assistant messages until find most recent set_todos with non-terminal items') gives sticky in-progress plan across follow-ups, matching Cursor v1.4+.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: If user types 'wait, also do X for the first one,' agent's next turn might not re-emit set_todos and strip blinks empty.
findings.suggested_patch: Trivial loop change in aegis-todos.ts.

findings.claim: ≥2 discrete actions threshold under-specified. Will produce false negatives AND false positives. Proposed copy contradicts itself: ≥2 discrete actions vs reserve todos for user-visible workstreams vs excludes single list_project_issues — but canonical Fix flow is 4 tool calls for ONE deliverable.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Existing prompt rule 9: single fix is already 4 sequential tool calls. Rule needs unit-of-counting definition: 'discrete actions' = user-visible deliverables, NOT tool calls.
findings.suggested_patch: Rewrite around user-visible deliverables. Add 2 concrete YES/NO examples in prompt itself.

findings.claim: 'Don't return prose to the user until the list is fully done' directly conflicts with chat UX expectations and existing prompt's tone rules. On 5-item list this means 60+ seconds of dead silence with only the strip flipping icons.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Existing system prompt: 'Be concise and direct... Match response shape to the question.' Cursor / Claude Code: BOTH emit per-item commentary.
findings.suggested_patch: Replace with: 'Treat the todo strip as canonical progress indicator — do NOT narrate "Now I'll do step 1..." The strip already shows that. Per item, may emit at most one short prose result. After final update_todo({done}) may emit one summary paragraph if helpful.'

findings.claim: Proposed rule overlaps and contradicts the just-added 'Plan revisions' fan-out rule. Existing rule prescribes multi-revise without todos; new rule says 'call set_todos before first real tool call' — incompatible (parallel vs sequential).
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Existing system-prompt.ts ~line 78: 'fan out — don't ask first, just make calls.' Plan sample: 'before the first real tool call... before each item, update_todo({in_progress}).'
findings.suggested_patch: Cross-reference: 'For multi-plan revisions, call set_todos first with one todo per plan, then issue all revise_fix calls in parallel, emit update_todo({done}) for each as result lands.'

findings.claim: Plan acknowledges LLM-fabricated kebab-case ids are a risk but proposed prompt rule gives no concrete rule for generating stable ids.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Schema description: 'Stable kebab-case identifier you'll use in update_todo' — model will fabricate inconsistently. Existing prompt rule 2: 'Never invent identifiers' applies to tool-output ids; set_todos REQUIRES model to invent them.
findings.suggested_patch: Add to prompt: 'Generate todo id as lowercase-kebab-case from title. When calling update_todo, COPY THE ID VERBATIM from set_todos earlier in turn — do not regenerate.' Add server-side validation in v1.1: walk current-turn parts on update_todo execute and return {error: 'unknown todo id'} so model self-corrects.

findings.claim: Plan asserts 'agent should call set_todos again with new plan' for mid-flight redirect, but proposed prompt copy says only '...until list is fully done OR the user redirects you' with no actual instruction.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Three plausible behaviors with no guidance.
findings.suggested_patch: Add: 'If user changes scope mid-list, first emit update_todo({status:cancelled}) for any in-flight todo abandoning, then call set_todos with revised full list. Reuse ids from prior list where work unchanged.'

findings.claim: Plan cites Cursor and Claude Code but proposed prompt copy doesn't actually borrow their language patterns. Both use imperative scaffolding ('Use this tool when...', 'Do NOT use this tool when...') with bulleted examples; Deptex copy is one paragraph.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Existing system prompt follows Claude-Code-leaked-prompt scaffolding pattern.
findings.suggested_patch: Restructure rule as When/Do-NOT/Lifecycle scaffold mirroring Claude Code's TaskCreate prompt structure.

findings.claim: Plan validates input via jsonSchema but doesn't constrain or sanitize user-visible title and reason fields. Schema permits 120 chars title / 240 chars reason of arbitrary text.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: If model has been fed user-pasted advisory text, could carry attacker-crafted content into title that renders verbatim.
findings.suggested_patch: Add to schema: title regex (printable chars only); strip control chars on render. Add to prompt: 'Phrase todo titles in your own words — do NOT copy raw text from advisories.'

findings.claim: Task says 'Add new bullet under rule 9 (or its own rule 10).' Rule 9 is exclusively Fix flow; nesting generic multi-step orchestration rule under it mis-files conceptually.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Rules 6 (parallelize), 7 (chain naturally) are orchestration rules. Generic orchestration belongs near 6/7, not after Fix flow.
findings.suggested_patch: Place new rule as rule 8 (between 'If a tool fails, say so' and Fix flow).

findings.claim: Tool result is {ok:true} with no schema enforcement on AI-emitted args. Plan does not specify what happens if a part has malformed args.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan derivation: '(parts[lastSetIdx].args?.todos ?? []).map(...)' — silent fallback.
findings.suggested_patch: Add zod parsing in deriveTodos: parse args against TodosArgs schema; on failure log console.warn and return [] — no-crash but visible to dogfood.

findings.claim: Plan asserts 'No changes to aegis_tool_executions' but every tool call goes through agent.ts onStepFinish which unconditionally writes to aegis_tool_executions via saveToolExecution.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: agent.ts:87-108 onStepFinish iterates toolCalls and calls saveToolExecution for every one with hardcoded toolCategory: 'read_only', permissionLevel: 'safe'. No exclusion list. Multi-step todo turn writes 10+ rows.
findings.suggested_patch: Add CHAT_ONLY_TOOLS allowlist in agent.ts onStepFinish OR add property audit?: false on AegisToolEntry and respect in onStepFinish.

findings.claim: Plan ignores agent runs with stopWhen: stepCountIs(25) — every set_todos and update_todo eats one of those 25 steps. 6 todos × (1 set + 6 in_progress + 6 done) = 13 calls leaving 12 for actual work.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: agent.ts:77 stopWhen: stepCountIs(25). Plan caps todos at maxItems: 12; 12 todos × (1 + 12 + 12) = 25 — exactly the cap.
findings.suggested_patch: Either (a) bump stepCountIs to 50, (b) lower maxItems to 6, or (c) collapse update_todo into batch update_todos({updates: [{id, status}]}).

findings.claim: Plan punts on where in system prompt the new tools/rule slot. Category list is flat enumeration; jamming set_todos into 'Fix' is wrong.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: system-prompt.ts:33-43 categories don't include orchestration. Rule 9 is canonical fix sequence — adding todos confuses canonical flow.
findings.suggested_patch: Add NEW category 'Plan' as last entry: '**Plan**: set_todos, update_todo'. Add NEW rule 10 'Multi-step plans'.

findings.plan_section: Files this feature will touch / MessageBubble suppression
findings.claim: Plan says 'add early returns'. Naive 'if (toolName === set_todos) return' inside parts.forEach skips part WITHOUT calling flushTools first AND without consuming part. A stream of [set_todos, update_todo, update_todo] (no text/request_fix) results in zero buffered tools AND zero text — MessageBubble renders EMPTY bubble.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble.tsx:99-145 elements array fills only on text or non-suppressed tool. Wrapping div at 147-153 still renders, producing empty assistant bubble.
findings.suggested_patch: After parts.forEach + flushTools(), early-return null when elements.length === 0 AND error undefined. Add test case for parts array containing only set_todos/update_todo.

findings.claim: Derivation reads parts[i].args for tool-call parts but persisted shape uses args while LIVE streaming shape uses input. During streaming type will be tool-set_todos (not tool-call) and args field may be input. Strip will be invisible during streaming and only appear on persisted reload.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble.tsx:34-47 isToolPart checks BOTH dynamic-tool AND tool- prefix; toolNameFor strips tool-. backend/src/lib/aegis-v3/parts.ts:41 — persisted shape uses type: 'tool-call' + args. Live and persisted diverge.
findings.suggested_patch: Update deriveTodos to mirror MessageBubble's normalization: accept either part.toolName === 'set_todos' OR part.type === 'tool-set_todos'; read args from part.args ?? part.input. Add Jest test using live AI SDK streaming part shape.

findings.claim: Plan dismisses operating-mode. In 'announce' mode, agent supposed to narrate before destructive actions — new rule 'don't return prose until list fully done' contradicts announce-mode semantics.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: tool-types.ts:13 defines three modes; system-prompt.ts currently doesn't condition on mode but agent context has it via ctx.operatingMode.
findings.suggested_patch: Soften rule: 'Keep prose minimal while items pending. After completing each item, MAY add brief 1-line note.' OR explicitly skip chat-todos rule injection when ctx.operatingMode === 'announce'.

findings.claim: Tools return {ok: true} but existing fix tools use domain object or {error: string}. Plan doesn't explicitly set danger: 'safe' to match convention.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: fix.ts:482-488 check_fix_status uses danger: 'safe'.
findings.suggested_patch: Every entry in chat.ts should explicitly set danger: 'safe' (no permission key).

findings.claim: Plan doesn't address: if user has two browser tabs open on different threads, will strip rehydrate from persisted parts when switching back? Plan claims yes via 'page refresh restores state' but doesn't verify buildInitialMessages emits parts in shape deriveTodos expects.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: ChatPane.tsx:88 buildInitialMessages reconstructs UIMessages from DB rows. backend/src/lib/aegis-v3/parts.ts:41 persisted parts use type: 'tool-call' + args.
findings.suggested_patch: Add Acceptance criterion to Task 8: 'Switch threads mid-stream and verify strip rehydrates correctly on original thread when revisited.' Add test fixture using persisted shape.

findings.claim: set_todos (plural) + update_todo (singular) is asymmetric. Every other Aegis tool pair uses parallel naming. Model that sees set_todos once will frequently call update_todos plural by analogy — that tool doesn't exist and fails silently.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: fix.ts and others — naming is consistently parallel.
findings.suggested_patch: Rename to set_todos + update_todos (batch update API per finding f2) or set_todo_list + update_todo. Recommend former.

findings.claim: Plan stores state in tool-call args. When AI SDK sends previous turns BACK to model on next user message, args is what model sees — model may try to 'continue' previous list.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Plan: 'each new user message implicitly retires old list.' AI SDK behavior: tool-call args + results are part of message history sent on subsequent turns.
findings.suggested_patch: Add explicit instruction: 'Each user message starts fresh. Don't reference todos from previous turn unless user explicitly continues.'

findings.plan_section: Open questions / unused durable task system
findings.claim: Future feature that auto-spawns fixes from a sprint will produce both durable task list AND chat-todos strip with overlapping items.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: CLAUDE.md: 'Sprint orchestration: batch fix discovery and AI fix scheduling' uses durable path.
findings.suggested_patch: Add Risks note: 'When sprint-orchestration features land, todos for same workstream may appear in both surfaces.'

findings.claim: Plan declares 'No loading state' and reduces 'error state' to malformed-input crash protection. Never specifies what user sees when agent fails mid-list, refuses, model crashes, or user hits Stop. Strip will sit indefinitely with Loader2 spinning.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: User asks 'fix these 5'. Strip appears with 5 rows. Item 2 in_progress. Model errors / rate-limits / user hits Stop. Chat shows error bubble in scroll, but sticky strip keeps Loader2 animating forever — promising work that's been abandoned.
findings.suggested_patch: Add 'stalled' visibility rule: if message is latest assistant message AND useChat status !== 'streaming' AND any todo non-terminal, render those rows in muted 'stalled' style with Pause icon, 'Stream ended' caption, and Retry button. Stop button must clear in_progress visuals.

findings.claim: Strip lives in bottom dock sticky to composer. With 12-item list mid-update, strip eats large chunk of viewport — exactly when user scrolls up to re-read original context.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: 12-row strip + 'Plan 3/12' header + SendQueuePanel + ChatInput occupy ~350-450px of viewport bottom. On 13" laptop ~40% of window blocking exactly the message user wanted to re-read. No collapse affordance.
findings.suggested_patch: Add collapse/expand control. Auto-collapse to one-line view when todos > 5. Specify max strip height (max-h-[40vh] overflow-y-auto).

findings.claim: Plan acknowledges 'agent might forget to call update_todo' as highest risk but offers no UX recovery affordance. User left staring at permanently in_progress row with no way to dismiss, mark done, and 'no user editing in v1' explicitly forbids interaction.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Agent emits update_todo(item-3, done) forgetting item-2. Agent's prose says 'I revised both files.' User sees strip stuck on item-2: in_progress next to item-3: done — contradicting agent's own message.
findings.suggested_patch: v1 must include minimal recovery: when stream ended and any todo non-terminal, show subtle 'Dismiss' (X) on strip header that clears strip for this turn (client-side only). Per-row hover affordance to mark done/cancelled manually.

findings.plan_section: Risks > Open question on terminal-state visibility
findings.claim: Whether strip shows 'Done.' pill or vanishes immediately is TBD, but success criteria require user to understand multi-step task completed. Vanishing-without-pause means user blinks, strip is gone.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: Final update_todo fires; visibility rule flips to false; strip unmounts immediately. User watching strip count up '3/5 → 4/5 → 5/5' loses only persistent completion artifact at moment of success.
findings.suggested_patch: Resolve open question in plan: hold strip ~1.5-2s after all-terminal with 'Done — N/N' confirmation row.

findings.claim: System-prompt rule says ≥2 discrete actions but neither tool description nor prompt defines 'discrete'. First time agent emits set_todos for one-shot fix, strip pops in for ~3s and vanishes — pure noise. Conversely, missing it for a legit 2-step request, user assumes feature broken.
findings.evidence_or_alternative: User types 'fix the SQL injection in payments.ts'. Agent: read file, draft patch, open PR — 3 tool calls. Does agent emit 3-item todo? Plan says no but 'trivial' is undefined.
findings.suggested_patch: Tighten rule with concrete heuristic in both prompt AND tool description: 'Only call set_todos when user-visible workstreams take ≥30s each AND there are ≥2 of them.' Add 2-3 positive/negative examples directly to system prompt.
